write better:
In this section, the characteristics of the modes identified by the SSI COV method have been extracted and compared.
In this section, we have delineated and contrasted the attributes of the modes unveiled through the Stochastic Subspace Identification (SSI) COV technique.
write like a structural engineer would

In this section, the modal properties identified utilizing the System Identification technique, specifically the Covariance-driven Stochastic Subsystem Identification (SSI-COV) approach, are extracted and subjected to comparative analysis.

write better:
Stability diagram of identifying stable modes obtained from the SSI COV shock response method (top) and their clustering after cleaning the diagram by applying MPC and MPD indices (bottom)

The stability diagram illustrates the stable modes as discerned through the SSI-COV shock response method, shown at the top. Following refinement of the diagram utilizing Mode Pairing Consistency (MPC) and Modal Phase Difference (MPD) indices, the bottom section reveals the resultant clustering of modes for enhanced clarity and precision.
its the title of a figure
Stability Diagrams: Initial Identification of Stable Modes via SSI-COV Shock Response Method (Top) and Post-Cleansing Clustering with MPC and MPD Indices (Bottom)

in modal analysis context, write this better and convincing:
Between closely spaced modes of 5-6, and 7-8, modes 5 and 8 have been selected since they were activated in model orders.

In the context of modal analysis, where closely spaced modal pairs 5-6 and 7-8 were examined, modes 5 and 8 have been preferentially chosen due to their consistent activation across multiple model orders, suggesting a higher degree of physical significance and reliability in these particular modes.

is this good?
Closely spaced modal pairs (5,6), (7,8) were examined further. Modes five and eight have been preferentially chosen due to their consistent activation across lower model orders, suggesting a higher degree of physical significance and reliability.

Yes, the revised statement is well-articulated and clear in the context of modal analysis. It concisely communicates the rationale behind selecting specific modes based on their appearance across lower model orders, which indicates their physical relevance and dependability in the analysis.

MAC matrix and the resulting frequencies (Hz) after removing false modes
Refined Frequencies in Hertz (Hz) Derived from the MAC Matrix Following Elimination of Spurious Modes

paragraph:
Closely spaced modal pairs (5,6), (7,8) were examined further. Modes five and eight have been preferentially chosen due to their consistent activation across lower model orders, suggesting a higher degree of physical significance and reliability.
this is figure title coming after:
MAC matrix and the resulting frequencies (Hz) following elimination of spurious modes

add a sentence after the paragraph for the figure

Closely spaced modal pairs (5,6) and (7,8) underwent further scrutiny. Modes five and eight have been selectively identified due to their recurrent emergence across lower model orders, indicating a more substantial degree of physical pertinence and dependability in these modes. The ensuing figure, titled "MAC Matrix and Resulting Frequencies (Hz) Following Elimination of Spurious Modes," visually encapsulates the refined modal frequencies post exclusion of non-physical modes, thereby providing a clearer representation of the structural dynamics.

write this better:
The resulted modal parameters are presented in Figure 14
The modal parameters determined from the analysis are depicted in Figure 14.

write this better:
The modal parameters determined from the modal procedure explained earlier are depicted in Figure 14.
Figure 14 showcases the modal parameters derived from the previously described modal analysis procedure.

rewrite better:
Closely spaced modal pairs (5,6), (7,8) were examined further. Modes five and eight have been preferentially chosen due to their consistent activation across lower model orders, suggesting a higher degree of physical significance and reliability. Figure 13 visually encapsulates the refined modal frequencies post exclusion of spurious modes, thereby providing a clearer representation of the physical modes of the bridge.

Upon detailed examination of the closely spaced modal pairs (5,6) and (7,8), modes five and eight were selected for their persistent presence across the lower model orders, indicating their greater physical relevancy and dependability. Figure 13 presents a visual representation of the adjusted modal frequencies after the removal of erroneous modes, offering a more accurate depiction of the bridge's true modal characteristics.

Average Damping Ratios of Structural Modes Derived from the Matrix Pencil Method

i wanna write this in note section of the table, write it better:
DNF55 train moving from left to right with speed of 55 km/hr, DNR55 train moving from right to left with speed of 55 km/hr
Note: "DNF55" denotes a train traveling from left to right at a speed of 55 km/h, while "DNR55" represents a train moving from right to left at the same speed of 55 km/h.
can you write it more concise?
Note: "DNF55" indicates a train moving left to right at 55 km/h, and "DNR55" a train right to left at 55 km/h.

write better:
In this part, the modal damping ratio is estimated based on the logarithmic reduction method and based on the analysis of the acceleration record under free vibration after the locomotive passes. In the logarithmic reduction method, a digital crossover filter is applied to the acceleration record in the frequency range whose damping ratio is considered, and then an exponential function will be fitted to the peaks of the filtered record. The bandpass filter width was set to 0.2 Hz to avoid any other frequency interference that could be associated with the train-line (load) system or other bridge modes.

In this section, we estimate the modal damping ratio by applying the logarithmic decrement method to the analysis of the acceleration records captured during the free vibration phase following the passage of a locomotive. We utilize a digital bandpass filter on the acceleration data within the targeted frequency range to isolate the mode of interest. Subsequently, an exponential curve is fitted to the peak amplitudes of the filtered signal to determine the damping ratio. To ensure the purity of our modal analysis and mitigate any potential contamination from frequencies related to the train-track interaction or other bridge modes, we have meticulously set the bandpass filter's bandwidth to 0.2 Hz.
write from the 3rd pov

In this analysis, the estimation of modal damping ratios is conducted utilizing the logarithmic decrement method based on acceleration records during free vibration after a locomotive's transit. A digital bandpass filter is applied within a specific frequency range to the acceleration data to identify the mode being analyzed, followed by fitting an exponential function to the peaks within the filtered dataset. To preclude interference from other frequencies that may arise from the interaction of the train and track system, or from other modes of the bridge, the bandwidth of the bandpass filter is carefully set at 0.2 Hz.

it is the title of figure, write it better:
Estimation of damping ratio of the second vertical mode: (a) logarithmic reduction method and (b) matrix pencil method
Damping Ratio Estimation for the Second Vertical Mode: (a) Logarithmic Decrement Method and (b) Matrix Pencil Method
